author: @maxbiagi3091
text: As a printshop, in my experience price/performance this was a bad choice. Elegoo CC Is better, but if you want machine for longrun without head pain, pay little bit more for Bambulab P1S. It worth it.

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Awesome 🎉

author: @Mikehibbett
text: My first 3D printer was a "Bits & Bytes" v2. I helped with the firmware and got a kit for free, in return for a series of magazine articles about. I still have the first print I made, from ABS, which I now know is the worst material to use as a beginner. Thanks for bringing me in to the project Iain!

@ThinkingandTinkering My time is way more valuable than a few hundred bucks, and i will spend less time working on it, and get thousands of hours more trouble free printing on it that any kobra owner!

Cheers!

author: @removechan10298
text:
I agree, the combined unit is fantastic, and that will also help for print farms. Having a few of these where all your spools are dry and ready to go is fantastic. A multi-head might have been cool, but this looks really good.

I think you have to turn off the autofocus on your camera, :) looks squiffy !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Cheers mate, I am really impressed with the combo unit, and the autofocus is playing up again - I will try and sort it for the next video.

author: @johnwynne-qx6br
text: Good to see a new toy, good luck with it and have fun good review 👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thanks mate, appreciate that!

author: @northwind7409
text: Like you, I started 3D printing ten years ago, with a Prusa I3 clone. After many modifications, I finally got it doing everything well ( for a 10-year old design). Last Wednesday, I received my new Q2 printer - very similar to what you just showed us. What a difference ten years makes! Right now, I'm printing the first set of plates for cardboard spools. So much faster than the old one. I'll have to wait a couple of months for the feeder box. That gives me lots of time to learn the machine. And here I thought I was pretty much done with adding more tools.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: not quite sure you are ever done mate lol
